name: wild-time

env_axis.name: time
env_axis.shift_mechanism: 5 sub-datasets each with own temporal-drift mechanism (fashion / satellite-imaging-tech / clinical-practice / news-editorial / scientific-field)

schema.suite_note: suite — 5 sub-datasets (Yearbook, FMoW, MIMIC-prognosis, HuffPost, arXiv). Each has its own profile and pair. Under the regime scheme, 'blob' covers image and text uniformly.

facts: 5 sub-datasets (Yearbook, FMoW, MIMIC-prognosis, HuffPost, arXiv) sharing a chronological eval protocol; NeurIPS 2022; multi-task per sub-dataset (MIMIC = readmission + mortality).
hypotheses: Time-as-environment shifts are not mechanistically consistent across the 5 sub-datasets; each has its own incompatible drift mechanism rather than a shared method ordering at any given epoch.
The suite's published 'time generalization' claim holds only on the simpler sub-datasets (Yearbook) rather than uniformly across all 5.
intuitions: A Wild-Time leaderboard with 2024-25 updates and foundation-model results on Yearbook likely exists.
Cross-comparisons with WILDS-Time and the TGB temporal axis probably exist (these are distinct projects despite naming overlap).
unknowns: 'Temporal shift' is treated as a single primitive, but the underlying mechanism differs categorically across the 5 sub-datasets (fashion / satellite-tech / clinical-practice / editorial / scientific-field); there is no established label for shift-mechanism heterogeneity within a single environment axis. The Wild-Time framing presupposes their unification.
epistemic_state.boundary: A suite requiring descent to sub-dataset manifests before routing methods; suite-level claims about 'temporal generalization' do not hold without sub-dataset agreement.

representational_commitments: Time-as-env (suite-level commitment, untested for consistency across sub-datasets)
5 specific sub-datasets chosen as representative — selection itself is curatorial
Multi-task per sub-dataset (MIMIC readmission + mortality) bundled
MIMIC sub-dataset uses Wild-Time's derived clinical-features version, not raw MIMIC-III/IV (which are user-excluded from this corpus)
materialization_status: 4 of 5 Wild-Time sub-datasets via Google Drive (per gdown file IDs hardcoded in wildtime/data/utils.py): Yearbook, FMoW, HuffPost, arXiv (2.5 GB alone), plus Drug-TDC. ~4.3 GB total. MIMIC requires PhysioNet credentialing (excluded).
